findings.title: Zero marketmind imports — no back-import risk
findings.description: Imports: math, dataclasses, datetime (all stdlib). Zero imports from marketmind. No import boundary violations possible.

findings.title: Clean public API: two functions, one dataclass
findings.description: Public interface: fit_platt_scaling(confidences, outcomes, max_iter) -> PlattCoefficients, apply_platt_scaling(confidence, coeffs) -> float. Single responsibility: confidence calibration via Platt scaling. No side effects. No module-level state. Returns immutable dataclass.

findings.title: Module is a leaf node in the DAG
findings.description: platt_scaling depends on nothing in marketmind. It is depended on by: calibration tracking (Layer 5), which passes it to LearningStore.update_calibration(). No circular dependency possible. Correct position as a utility module at the bottom of the dependency graph.

findings.title: PlattCoefficients dataclass includes pre/post ECE for audit trail
findings.description: The dataclass captures pre_calibration_ece and post_calibration_ece, enabling verification that calibration improved (or didn't). This supports the Phase I goal of measurable self-improvement. Good instrumentation design.

findings.title: Backward compatibility: new module, no existing consumers to break
findings.description: This is a new Phase I module. No existing code imports it. The interface (fit → PlattCoefficients, apply → float) is stable and unlikely to need breaking changes.

notes: Clean leaf module with no dependencies, well-defined interface, and good instrumentation. Zero integration risk.
